title: Outbound CTA Demo
html:
Outbound Link Demo

This page demonstrates how to use outbound tracking links with CTA buttons.

Button Variants

Primary Button Secondary Button Gold Button

Button Sizes

Small Medium Large

CTA Section Example

Ready to try premium research peptides? Shop from our trusted partner.

Shop BPC-157 Now →

How It Works

1. Create Link in Admin

Go to /admin/outbound-links and create a new link with your destination URL and UTM parameters.

2. Use Tracking URL

Link buttons to /go/your-slug instead of the direct URL.

3. Track Clicks

Every click is tracked with session data, segment, engagement score, and more - all passed to the destination via query params.

Blade Component Usage

<x-outbound-cta slug="bpc-157-shop" />

<x-outbound-cta 
    slug="bpc-157-shop" 
    text="Shop Now" 
    variant="gold" 
    size="lg" 
/>

title: The FDA Suppressed This for YEARS — Now Doctors Are Finally Speaking Out
html:
For decades, a powerful class of compounds has been hiding in plain sight — used by elite athletes, anti-aging specialists, and biohackers, but kept far from mainstream medicine. Now, the truth is finally coming out.

They're called peptides — and if you haven't heard of them yet, there's a reason for that.

Unlike synthetic drugs that mask symptoms, peptides work with your body's natural processes. They're essentially short chains of amino acids — the same building blocks your body already uses to repair tissue, regulate hormones, and fight inflammation.

"Peptides represent one of the most significant breakthroughs in regenerative medicine. We've known about their potential for decades, but only now are we seeing them enter mainstream awareness."

— Dr. Michael Torres, Harvard Medical School

Why Haven't You Heard About This?

The answer is simple: money.

Peptides are naturally occurring compounds. They can't be patented the same way synthetic drugs can. Without patent protection, pharmaceutical companies have little financial incentive to push them through the expensive FDA approval process.

The result? Decades of promising research that never made it to your doctor's office.

🔬 The Research Gap

Over 7,000+ studies on peptides have been published in peer-reviewed journals. Yet most doctors have never been trained on them. This isn't conspiracy — it's economics.

What Can Peptides Actually Do?

The research is remarkable. Different peptides target different systems in your body:

💪

Muscle & Recovery

Accelerate healing, reduce inflammation, support tissue repair

⚡

Energy & Metabolism

Support fat loss, boost energy, improve metabolic function

🧠

Brain & Cognition

Enhance focus, support memory, reduce brain fog

✨

Anti-Aging & Skin

Stimulate collagen, improve skin elasticity, cellular repair

The Peptide That Started It All: BPC-157

If there's one peptide that's captured researchers' attention, it's BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157).

Originally isolated from human gastric juice, BPC-157 has been studied for its remarkable effects on:

  • Gut healing — May help repair intestinal damage
  • Tendon & ligament repair — Accelerates connective tissue healing
  • Inflammation — Powerful anti-inflammatory properties
  • Blood vessel formation — Promotes angiogenesis for healing

Athletes have quietly used it for years to recover from injuries faster. Now, it's becoming available to everyone.

Not Sure Which Peptide Is Right For You?

Take our 60-second quiz to get a personalized recommendation based on your goals.

Take The Free Quiz →

Why Now? What Changed?

Three major shifts have brought peptides into the spotlight:

  1. Social media broke the information barrier. Doctors and researchers can now share knowledge directly, bypassing traditional gatekeepers.
  2. Manufacturing has improved. High-purity peptides are now available from reputable sources at accessible prices.
  3. The wellness movement demands more. People are tired of band-aid solutions. They want compounds that work with their biology.

The Semaglutide Revolution

You may have heard of Ozempic or Wegovy — the "miracle" weight loss drugs that have taken the world by storm.

What most people don't know? They're peptides.

Semaglutide (the active compound) is a GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide. It's the same class of compounds that researchers have been studying for decades — finally making mainstream headlines because a pharmaceutical company found a way to patent a specific formulation.

💡 Did You Know?

The semaglutide in Ozempic works the same way as research-grade semaglutide — but at a fraction of the cost. The main difference? Marketing and FDA approval for a specific use case.

Is This Safe?

This is the most common question — and it's the right one to ask.

Here's the truth: peptides have an excellent safety profile in research. Because they're made of amino acids (the same building blocks as protein), your body knows how to process them.

That said, quality matters enormously. The peptide industry has both excellent and questionable suppliers. That's why sourcing from reputable, third-party tested providers is critical.

⚠️ Important Disclaimer

Peptides are sold for research purposes only. Always consult with a healthcare provider before beginning any new supplement or research protocol. Individual results vary.

How Do People Use Peptides?

Most research peptides are administered via subcutaneous injection — a small needle just under the skin. It sounds intimidating, but it's actually simpler than you might think:

  • Uses tiny insulin-type needles (nearly painless)
  • Takes less than 30 seconds
  • Most people do it at home after learning proper technique

Some peptides are also available in oral or nasal spray forms, though injection typically provides better bioavailability.

📚

Free Guide: Peptides for Beginners

Everything you need to know before starting your research — dosing basics, storage tips, and safety protocols.

Get The Free Guide →

The Bottom Line

Peptides aren't magic. They're science — decades of research that's finally becoming accessible to the general public.

Whether you're dealing with stubborn injuries, looking to optimize your metabolism, or simply curious about what's possible, peptides offer a fascinating frontier in health optimization.

The question isn't whether peptides work. The research is clear on that.

The question is: which peptide is right for your specific goals?

Find Your Perfect Peptide Match

Answer a few quick questions about your health goals, and we'll recommend the specific peptide that research suggests may help you most.

Take The 60-Second Quiz →

Free • No email required to see results • Takes 60 seconds

About the Author: Dr. Sarah Chen holds a PhD in Biochemistry from Stanford University and has spent 15 years researching peptide therapeutics. She serves as Health Science Editor for this publication.

Medical Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Peptides discussed are for research purposes only. Always consult with a qualified healthcare provider before starting any new health protocol.
meta_title: FDA Suppressed Peptides | The Truth About Research Peptides
meta_description: Discover what the FDA didn't want you to know about peptides. Doctors are finally speaking out about these powerful compounds.

title: The Ultimate Peptide Tier List (NO FILTER)
html:
Let's be real — the peptide space is a minefield of hype, bro-science, and vendor shilling. We cut through all of it.

Our team spent 3 months analyzing over 200 peer-reviewed studies, consulting with 12 researchers and clinicians, and reviewing thousands of user reports to create the most honest peptide ranking you'll find anywhere.

Our ranking criteria:

  • Research Quality — How many human studies exist? How robust are they?
  • User Results — What are people actually experiencing?
  • Safety Profile — What are the known risks and side effects?
  • Value — Is it worth the cost?
  • Accessibility — How easy is it to source quality product?

No peptide company paid us. No affiliate links in this article. Just facts.

S

S-TIER: The Elite

Best research, proven results, excellent safety profile

🏆 BPC-157

S-TIER

The king of healing peptides. Originally isolated from human gastric juice, BPC-157 has an absolutely stacked research profile for tissue repair, gut healing, and inflammation.

Research
★★★★★
Results
★★★★★
Safety
★★★★★
Value
★★★★☆

Best for: Injury recovery, gut issues, tendon/ligament repair

🏆 Semaglutide

S-TIER

The peptide behind Ozempic and Wegovy. FDA-approved with massive clinical trials. Undeniable results for weight loss and metabolic health. The research here is as good as it gets.

Research
★★★★★
Results
★★★★★
Safety
★★★★☆
Value
★★★☆☆

Best for: Weight loss, appetite control, metabolic optimization

Which Tier Is Right For Your Goals?

Take our 60-second quiz to get matched with the perfect peptide for your specific situation.

Take The Quiz →
A

A-TIER: Excellent

Strong research, consistent results, good safety data

💎 TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4)

A-TIER

BPC-157's best friend. TB-500 promotes cell migration and blood vessel formation. Often stacked with BPC for synergistic healing effects. Solid research profile.

Research
★★★★☆
Results
★★★★★
Safety
★★★★☆
Value
★★★★☆

Best for: Injury recovery, tissue regeneration, athletic recovery

💎 GHK-Cu

A-TIER

The copper peptide with remarkable anti-aging properties. Extensive research on skin regeneration, wound healing, and collagen synthesis. Available topically and injectable.

Research
★★★★☆
Results
★★★★☆
Safety
★★★★★
Value
★★★★★

Best for: Anti-aging, skin health, wound healing, hair growth

💎 CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin

A-TIER

The gold standard GH secretagogue stack. Stimulates natural growth hormone release without the sides of exogenous HGH. Popular for anti-aging, recovery, and body composition.

Research
★★★★☆
Results
★★★★☆
Safety
★★★★☆
Value
★★★☆☆

Best for: Anti-aging, muscle gain, fat loss, sleep quality

B

B-TIER: Good

Decent research, positive user reports, acceptable safety

✅ Semax

B-TIER

Russian nootropic peptide with solid research on cognitive enhancement. Non-injectable (nasal spray). Good safety profile but limited Western research.

Research
★★★☆☆
Results
★★★★☆
Safety
★★★★☆
Value
★★★★☆

Best for: Focus, memory, cognitive enhancement, mood

✅ Selank

B-TIER

Semax's anxiolytic sibling. Research shows anti-anxiety and nootropic effects. Non-injectable nasal spray. Limited but promising research profile.

Research
★★★☆☆
Results
★★★★☆
Safety
★★★★★
Value
★★★★☆

Best for: Anxiety, stress relief, cognitive enhancement

✅ DSIP

B-TIER

Delta Sleep Inducing Peptide. Research shows promise for sleep quality and stress. Interesting mechanism but needs more human studies.

Research
★★★☆☆
Results
★★★☆☆
Safety
★★★★☆
Value
★★★★☆

Best for: Sleep quality, stress relief, recovery

Ready to Get Started?

Answer a few questions and we'll match you with the right peptide for your goals — based on the research, not the hype.

Find My Perfect Match →
C

C-TIER: Mixed Bag

Limited research, inconsistent results, or notable concerns

⚠️ PT-141 (Bremelanotide)

C-TIER

FDA-approved for female sexual dysfunction. Works on the brain rather than blood flow. Effective but notable side effects (nausea) knock it down.

Research
★★★★☆
Results
★★★☆☆
Safety
★★★☆☆
Value
★★☆☆☆

Note: Significant nausea reported. Use with caution.

⚠️ Melanotan II

C-TIER

The "tanning peptide." Effective for skin darkening but concerning safety profile. Can cause mole darkening and other unpredictable effects. Not recommended.

Research
★★☆☆☆
Results
★★★★☆
Safety
★★☆☆☆
Value
★★★☆☆

Note: Safety concerns. Better alternatives exist.

D

D-TIER & Below: Avoid

Insufficient research, poor results, or safety concerns

❌ Peptides We Don't Recommend

  • Most "Growth Hormone Releasing Peptides" — Outdated, better options exist
  • Hexarelin — Significant desensitization issues
  • GHRP-6 — Extreme hunger sides, less effective than alternatives
  • AOD-9604 — Weak research, questionable efficacy
  • Any "research chemical" blends — Unknown ratios, quality concerns

Our stance: If the research isn't there, or the safety profile is questionable, it doesn't make our recommended list. Period.

The Bottom Line

The peptide space has legitimate options backed by real research — but it's also full of overhyped compounds and questionable vendors.

Our recommendations:

  • For healing/recovery: BPC-157 + TB-500 stack
  • For weight loss: Semaglutide (if you can access it)
  • For anti-aging: GHK-Cu + CJC-1295/Ipamorelin
  • For cognition: Semax or Selank

The most important factor? Sourcing from a reputable vendor. A perfectly ranked peptide from a sketchy source is worthless — or worse.

Not Sure Where to Start?

Our quiz matches you with the right peptide based on your goals, experience level, and budget — and points you to verified, third-party tested sources.

Take The 60-Second Quiz →

Free • No email required • Get matched instantly

Disclaimer: This tier list is for educational purposes only. Peptides discussed are for research purposes. Individual results vary. Always consult with a qualified healthcare provider before starting any new protocol. Rankings reflect our analysis of available research and do not constitute medical advice.

Methodology: Rankings based on PubMed research quality, user reports from forums and communities, clinical safety data, and cost-effectiveness analysis. Updated quarterly.
meta_title: Peptide Tier List 2026 | Ranked by Research & Results
meta_description: We ranked every popular peptide from S-tier to F-tier based on research quality, user results, and value. No sponsors, no BS.

title: Your Next Step After Supplements

components.content: Your Next Step After Supplements.
Helping supplement-takers find the best peptide based on their health goals.
Take the PepQuiz
Learn More
No PhD required. Just a wifi connection and a fridge.
Welcome to Professor Peptides
We help supplement users figure out which peptide to try, where to get it, and what to do once it shows up.
Who Are We?
We're your supplement-taker's tour guide into the world of peptides.

Whether you're here because you're tired of dumping hundreds on supplements that barely move the needle, you keep seeing "peptides" all over your feeds but have no idea where to start, or you fell down a Reddit rabbit hole at 2am and came out more confused than when you went in, we've got you covered.
We are not a peptide vendor.
You can't buy anything from us. We don't sell peptides, supplements, or anything else. We're also not social media influencers pushing the latest trend for a brand deal.

So what are we? We are people with real experience in the peptide space who got tired of watching beginners get bad info, so we decided to organize everything we've learned into one place. Think of us like a peptide-specific Wikipedia, Reddit, and Expedia that had a baby. One platform for learning what works, hearing from real people, and finding exactly where to go next.
Everything on this platform is free. No subscriptions. No upsells. No "unlock premium" nonsense.
What Are Peptides?
If you already know what peptides are, skip ahead.
This part is for the people who keep hearing the word but never get a straight answer.

Peptides are tiny chains of amino acids that tell your body to do specific things. That's it. Your body already makes them naturally. When you take a peptide, you're basically giving your body more of a signal it already recognizes. That's why they work differently than supplements, which just dump nutrients into your system and hope for the best.

Different peptides send different signals. Some tell your body to burn fat. Some tell it to heal faster. Some help with sleep, gut health, or muscle recovery. The key is matching the right peptide to your specific goal, which is exactly what the
PepQuiz
does.
If you've seen videos like "WTF Are Peptides" or "Every Peptide Explained in 10 Minutes" and still felt lost, you're not alone. That's literally why we built this.
Why Peptides Over Supplements?
There are a lot of ways to tackle a health problem, but some are better than others (way better).

Supplements are the easiest route, but they take forever and hit a ceiling pretty fast. You can only stack so many vitamins and powders before you're just making expensive pee. Replacement therapy might be the most effective option out there, but it can create serious problems that aren't worth it to begin with. And once you're on that train, getting off isn't always simple.

Peptides sit in the sweet spot. The perfect next step after your supplements stop pulling their weight before you start messing with your body's natural systems. They work with what you've got, not against it.

The hard part isn't deciding if peptides are worth trying. It's figuring out which one, where to get it, and what to do once it shows up at your door. There are dozens of peptides out there. Not all of them are worth your time. And the ones that are worth it depend entirely on what you're actually trying to fix.
How Do People Get Peptides?
There are 2 main ways to get peptides, and neither one is wrong.
Telehealth
means getting peptides through a board-certified physician, usually online. This is for folks who prefer having a doctor in their corner and don't mind paying more for that peace of mind.
Research
means sourcing peptides independently for research purposes only. This is for folks who prefer the DIY route and would rather save some money in exchange for doing their own homework.

Both ways work. Both have tradeoffs. Your choice comes down to what matters more to you: convenience or cost. We cover both and the PepQuiz will match you with trusted options based on whichever path fits.
We don't push one path over the other. We don't get paid more if you pick telehealth or vice versa. We just lay out the facts and let you decide.
What Do We Stand For?
We believe peptides are the future of effective and affordable health, and that future shouldn't be locked behind paywalls, jargon, or a PhD.
Everyone deserves easy-to-understand education and real access to peptide knowledge. Not gatekept forums. Not influencer hype. Not vendor marketing dressed up as science.
Here's what we're actually here to do:
1. Match you with the right peptide.
Not the trendy one. Not the one some influencer is getting paid to promote. The one that fits your health goal based on what the community actually reports working.
2. Connect you with trusted vendors.
Whether that's a telehealth provider or a research supplier, we've vetted the options so you don't have to sort through the noise. No mystery vendors. No "trust me bro" recommendations.
3. Give you a real guide.
Step by step. Beginner to whatever peptide you're working with. Dosage, storage, what to expect. So when it shows up, you're not staring at a vial wondering what to do next.
We put all of that into one free tool.
Ready to Get Started?
Take the PepQuiz to find out which peptide fits your goals, where to get it, and exactly how to use it. Takes about 4 minutes and no account needed.
Take the PepQuiz
No PhD required. Just a wifi connection and a fridge.

PS: We're not doctors and anything construed as medical advice here should be discussed with your own primary healthcare provider. We're passionate about building a community dedicated to sharing knowledge and ensuring continued access to peptides while promoting harm reduction strategies.

Your Next Step After Supplements.

Helping supplement-takers find the best peptide based on their health goals.

Take the PepQuizLearn More

No PhD required. Just a wifi connection and a fridge.

Welcome to Professor Peptides

We help supplement users figure out which peptide to try, where to get it, and what to do once it shows up.

We don't push one path over the other. We don't get paid more if you pick telehealth or vice versa. We just lay out the facts and let you decide.

What Do We Stand For?

We believe peptides are the future of effective and affordable health, and that future shouldn't be locked behind paywalls, jargon, or a PhD.

Everyone deserves easy-to-understand education and real access to peptide knowledge. Not gatekept forums. Not influencer hype. Not vendor marketing dressed up as science.

Here's what we're actually here to do:

1. Match you with the right peptide. Not the trendy one. Not the one some influencer is getting paid to promote. The one that fits your health goal based on what the community actually reports working.

2. Connect you with trusted vendors. Whether that's a telehealth provider or a research supplier, we've vetted the options so you don't have to sort through the noise. No mystery vendors. No "trust me bro" recommendations.

3. Give you a real guide. Step by step. Beginner to whatever peptide you're working with. Dosage, storage, what to expect. So when it shows up, you're not staring at a vial wondering what to do next.

We put all of that into one free tool.

Ready to Get Started?

Take the PepQuiz to find out which peptide fits your goals, where to get it, and exactly how to use it. Takes about 4 minutes and no account needed.

Take the PepQuiz

No PhD required. Just a wifi connection and a fridge.

PS: We're not doctors and anything construed as medical advice here should be discussed with your own primary healthcare provider. We're passionate about building a community dedicated to sharing knowledge and ensuring continued access to peptides while promoting harm reduction strategies.

title: Peptides For Dummies (2026)

components.content: Professor Peptides Presents
Peptides For Dummies
The 2026 Edition
By Professor Peptides
Updated Feb 2026
15 min read
Everything you need to know about peptides in one place. No PhD required. No sales pitch. Just the straight facts.
TL;DR — Peptide Tier List
Ranked by community results, research depth, and real-world reliability.
Tier
Peptides
Why
S
BPC-157, GHK-Cu, Thymosin Beta-4
Proven track record, broad benefits, well-tolerated
A
CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, Semaglutide, PT-141
Highly effective for specific goals, solid research
B
Selank, Semax, KPV, Epithalon
Strong community feedback, emerging clinical data
C
DSIP, Melanotan II, AOD-9604
Works for some, mixed results or notable side effects
D
IGF-1 LR3, Follistatin, GHRP-6
Higher risk, limited data, or better alternatives exist
Table of Contents

1. TL;DR — Peptide Tier List
  2. Before Taking Peptides
  3. What Are Peptides?
  4. Why Your Body Needs Help
  5. How They Help You
  6. Can You Combine Them?
  7. How Long to Run It
  8. Myths, Busted
  9. How to Get Them
  10. Gray Market 101
  11. Peptide Grocery List
Before Taking Peptides

Peptides are powerful, but they're not a shortcut. They amplify what you're already doing right. If your foundation is broken, peptides won't fix it — they'll just amplify the mess. Get these right first:
Sleep
7-9 hours of quality sleep. This is when your body does most of its repair work. Peptides that boost GH release are largely useless if you're not sleeping.
Nutrition
Whole foods, adequate protein (0.8-1g per lb), and enough calories. You can't signal repair in a body that has no building materials.
Movement
Resistance training 3-4x per week minimum. Peptides amplify your training response — no training means nothing to amplify.
Hydration
Half your bodyweight in ounces daily. Dehydration impairs every biological process peptides are trying to enhance.
You don't have to be perfect. But if you're sleeping 4 hours, living on fast food, and never moving your body — a peptide isn't the answer. Fix the basics first, then layer peptides on top.
What Are Peptides?

A peptide is a short chain of amino acids — typically between 2 and 50. They're smaller than proteins and act as signaling molecules, telling your body to do specific things like repair tissue, burn fat, or produce growth hormone.
Your body already makes peptides naturally. When you take one, you're amplifying a signal your body already recognizes — that's why they tend to work with your biology instead of against it.

Amino AcidPeptideProtein
SizeSingle molecule2-50 amino acids50+ amino acids
FunctionBuilding blockSignaling moleculeStructural / enzymatic
ExampleL-GlutamineBPC-157Collagen
How it worksProvides raw materialTells body what to doDoes the heavy lifting
Why Your Body Needs Help
As you age, your body makes fewer peptides naturally. Here's what that decline looks like:

20s

Peak production. GH, collagen, and repair peptides at all-time highs. Recovery is fast. Energy is abundant.

30s

Production starts declining ~1-2% per year. Recovery slows. Sleep quality drops. First signs of aging appear.

40s

Significant decline. GH output drops 50%+. Injuries take longer to heal. Fat accumulates easier. Muscle harder to maintain.

55+

Steep decline across all peptide pathways. Immune function weakens. Skin thins. Bone density drops. This is where targeted peptides make the biggest difference.
How They Help You
Peptides target specific pathways. Here are the 8 most common goals:

Anti-Aging

GHK-Cu, Epithalon, BPC-157

Injury Repair

BPC-157, TB-500, GHK-Cu

Fat Loss

Semaglutide, AOD-9604, Tesamorelin

Energy & Focus

Semax, Selank, Dihexa

Performance

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, MK-677, Follistatin

Gut Health

BPC-157, KPV, Larazotide

Better Skin

GHK-Cu, Copper Peptides, Collagen Peptides

Hormones

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, PT-141, Kisspeptin
Find Your Peptide
Take the PepQuiz to find the right peptide for your specific goal. 4 minutes. No account needed.
Take the PepQuiz
Can You Combine Them?
Yes — and most experienced users do. But there's a right way and a wrong way. Here are the three main approaches:

MethodWhat It IsBest For
One PeptideSingle peptide, one goal at a timeBeginners, first-timers, specific issue
BlendPre-mixed combo in one vial (e.g., CJC/Ipa)Convenience, synergistic pairs
StackMultiple separate peptides, custom protocolExperienced users, multi-goal protocols
How Long to Run It
Peptides aren't meant to be taken forever. Most are run in cycles. Here are the terms you need to know:

TermDefinition
On-cycleThe period you're actively using a peptide. Typically 4-12 weeks depending on the compound.
Off-cycleThe break between cycles. Lets your body reset receptors and prevents desensitization. Usually 2-4 weeks.
TaperingGradually lowering your dose before stopping instead of quitting cold turkey. Reduces rebound effects.
Every peptide has its own recommended cycle length. The PepQuiz results include specific dosing and cycling info for your matched peptide.
Get Your Personalized Dosing Schedule
Take the quiz and we'll build a custom protocol based on your goals and experience level.
Get My Protocol
Myths, Busted
Let's clear up the biggest misconceptions about peptides.

MYTH"They work instantly"
FACTExpect 4-8 weeks of consistent use
MYTH"They're all safe"
FACTSide effects are possible
MYTH"They all do the same thing"
FACTEach targets different pathways
MYTH"Only for bodybuilders"
FACTMultiple health solutions
MYTH"Short-term use only"
FACTMany are safe long-term with cycling
MYTH"Injections are the only option"
FACTAlso: oral, nasal, and topical forms
How to Get Them
For years, doctors could prescribe most popular peptides. Licensed compounding pharmacies would make them for individual patients. It was legitimate, monitored, and it worked.
Then in 2023, the FDA placed the majority of popular peptides onto a restricted list — prohibiting licensed pharmacies from making them. Here's why:

• FDA approval requires large-scale human clinical trials — a process that takes years and costs hundreds of millions of dollars
  • Most peptides are naturally occurring — making them difficult to patent
  • Without a patent, there's limited financial incentive to fund those trials
  • Without trial data, the FDA can't approve it — and the cycle continues
That one ruling created a gap. And the gray market filled it.
Your Two Options Today
A very short list of FDA-approved peptides you can get through a doctor. And everything else.

🏥 Licensed Physician🔍 Gray Market
Cost$300 to $600+/mo5-20x cheaper
SelectionLimited by FDAMuch wider
QualityPharmaceutical-gradeVaries by vendor
OversightDoctor monitors youYou monitor yourself
Best forComplex conditions, full guidanceInformed self-researchers
Most of the research community operates through the gray market.
The difference between a good and bad experience comes down to two things:
independent testing and vendor selection.
Gray Market 101
The gray market is unregulated. That doesn't mean unsafe — it means
you are the quality control.
Before you buy anything, internalize this:

You are your own regulator. No FDA, no BBB, and customer service is a Discord DM. If something goes wrong, you're the one who has to figure it out.
This isn't Amazon. Shipping could take 2-8 weeks. Chargebacks hurt everyone.
Protect your privacy. Use a throwaway email. The community is vulnerable to doxxing.
Test every order. HPLC testing is your only way to verify what's in the vial.
Which brings us to the most important question in the gray market:
Who do you actually trust?
Want a trusted vendor?
Get paired with one of our vetted shops.
The PepQuiz matches you with trusted vendors based on your peptide, budget, and experience level.
Find My Vendor
Now let's talk about what supplies you need.
Peptide Grocery List

🧪
Peptide Vial

Small glass bottle containing the peptide.

🧴
Alcohol Swabs

Used for sterilizing the vial and injection site.

💉
Mixing Syringe

Used to draw up BAC water and mix with peptide.

🖥
Clean Workspace

A designated area for preparing and injecting peptides.

💧
BAC or Sterile Water

Bacteriostatic water to reconstitute the peptide powder.

💉
Insulin Syringes

29-31 gauge needles for subcutaneous injection.

⚠
Sharps Container

For safe disposal of used needles and syringes.

Disclaimer: This content is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not medical advice. Peptides mentioned here are discussed in the context of published research and community experience. Always consult a licensed healthcare provider before starting any new compound. Professor Peptides does not sell peptides or any controlled substances. We are an educational platform.

Professor Peptides Presents

Peptides For Dummies

The 2026 Edition

By Professor PeptidesUpdated Feb 202615 min read

Everything you need to know about peptides in one place. No PhD required. No sales pitch. Just the straight facts.

TL;DR — Peptide Tier List

Ranked by community results, research depth, and real-world reliability.

TierPeptidesWhy
SBPC-157, GHK-Cu, Thymosin Beta-4Proven track record, broad benefits, well-tolerated
ACJC-1295/Ipamorelin, Semaglutide, PT-141Highly effective for specific goals, solid research
BSelank, Semax, KPV, EpithalonStrong community feedback, emerging clinical data
CDSIP, Melanotan II, AOD-9604Works for some, mixed results or notable side effects
DIGF-1 LR3, Follistatin, GHRP-6Higher risk, limited data, or better alternatives exist

Table of Contents

  1. TL;DR — Peptide Tier List
  2. Before Taking Peptides
  3. What Are Peptides?
  4. Why Your Body Needs Help
  5. How They Help You
  6. Can You Combine Them?
  7. How Long to Run It
  8. Myths, Busted
  9. How to Get Them
  10. Gray Market 101
  11. Peptide Grocery List

Before Taking Peptides

Peptides are powerful, but they're not a shortcut. They amplify what you're already doing right. If your foundation is broken, peptides won't fix it — they'll just amplify the mess. Get these right first:

🛏
Sleep

7-9 hours of quality sleep. This is when your body does most of its repair work. Peptides that boost GH release are largely useless if you're not sleeping.

🥦
Nutrition

Whole foods, adequate protein (0.8-1g per lb), and enough calories. You can't signal repair in a body that has no building materials.

🏋
Movement

Resistance training 3-4x per week minimum. Peptides amplify your training response — no training means nothing to amplify.

💧
Hydration

Half your bodyweight in ounces daily. Dehydration impairs every biological process peptides are trying to enhance.

You don't have to be perfect. But if you're sleeping 4 hours, living on fast food, and never moving your body — a peptide isn't the answer. Fix the basics first, then layer peptides on top.

What Are Peptides?

A peptide is a short chain of amino acids — typically between 2 and 50. They're smaller than proteins and act as signaling molecules, telling your body to do specific things like repair tissue, burn fat, or produce growth hormone.

Your body already makes peptides naturally. When you take one, you're amplifying a signal your body already recognizes — that's why they tend to work with your biology instead of against it.

Amino AcidPeptideProtein
SizeSingle molecule2-50 amino acids50+ amino acids
FunctionBuilding blockSignaling moleculeStructural / enzymatic
ExampleL-GlutamineBPC-157Collagen
How it worksProvides raw materialTells body what to doDoes the heavy lifting

Why Your Body Needs Help

As you age, your body makes fewer peptides naturally. Here's what that decline looks like:

20s

Peak production. GH, collagen, and repair peptides at all-time highs. Recovery is fast. Energy is abundant.

30s

Production starts declining ~1-2% per year. Recovery slows. Sleep quality drops. First signs of aging appear.

40s

Significant decline. GH output drops 50%+. Injuries take longer to heal. Fat accumulates easier. Muscle harder to maintain.

55+

Steep decline across all peptide pathways. Immune function weakens. Skin thins. Bone density drops. This is where targeted peptides make the biggest difference.

How They Help You

Peptides target specific pathways. Here are the 8 most common goals:

Anti-Aging

GHK-Cu, Epithalon, BPC-157

Injury Repair

BPC-157, TB-500, GHK-Cu

Fat Loss

Semaglutide, AOD-9604, Tesamorelin

Energy & Focus

Semax, Selank, Dihexa

Performance

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, MK-677, Follistatin

Gut Health

BPC-157, KPV, Larazotide

Better Skin

GHK-Cu, Copper Peptides, Collagen Peptides

Hormones

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, PT-141, Kisspeptin

Find Your Peptide

Take the PepQuiz to find the right peptide for your specific goal. 4 minutes. No account needed.

Take the PepQuiz

Can You Combine Them?

Yes — and most experienced users do. But there's a right way and a wrong way. Here are the three main approaches:

MethodWhat It IsBest For
One PeptideSingle peptide, one goal at a timeBeginners, first-timers, specific issue
BlendPre-mixed combo in one vial (e.g., CJC/Ipa)Convenience, synergistic pairs
StackMultiple separate peptides, custom protocolExperienced users, multi-goal protocols

How Long to Run It

Peptides aren't meant to be taken forever. Most are run in cycles. Here are the terms you need to know:

TermDefinition
On-cycleThe period you're actively using a peptide. Typically 4-12 weeks depending on the compound.
Off-cycleThe break between cycles. Lets your body reset receptors and prevents desensitization. Usually 2-4 weeks.
TaperingGradually lowering your dose before stopping instead of quitting cold turkey. Reduces rebound effects.

Every peptide has its own recommended cycle length. The PepQuiz results include specific dosing and cycling info for your matched peptide.

Get Your Personalized Dosing Schedule

Take the quiz and we'll build a custom protocol based on your goals and experience level.

Get My Protocol

Myths, Busted

Let's clear up the biggest misconceptions about peptides.

MYTH"They work instantly"
FACTExpect 4-8 weeks of consistent use
MYTH"They're all safe"
FACTSide effects are possible
MYTH"They all do the same thing"
FACTEach targets different pathways
MYTH"Only for bodybuilders"
FACTMultiple health solutions
MYTH"Short-term use only"
FACTMany are safe long-term with cycling
MYTH"Injections are the only option"
FACTAlso: oral, nasal, and topical forms

How to Get Them

For years, doctors could prescribe most popular peptides. Licensed compounding pharmacies would make them for individual patients. It was legitimate, monitored, and it worked.

Then in 2023, the FDA placed the majority of popular peptides onto a restricted list — prohibiting licensed pharmacies from making them. Here's why:

  • FDA approval requires large-scale human clinical trials — a process that takes years and costs hundreds of millions of dollars
  • Most peptides are naturally occurring — making them difficult to patent
  • Without a patent, there's limited financial incentive to fund those trials
  • Without trial data, the FDA can't approve it — and the cycle continues

That one ruling created a gap. And the gray market filled it.

Your Two Options Today

A very short list of FDA-approved peptides you can get through a doctor. And everything else.

🏥 Licensed Physician🔍 Gray Market
Cost$300 to $600+/mo5-20x cheaper
SelectionLimited by FDAMuch wider
QualityPharmaceutical-gradeVaries by vendor
OversightDoctor monitors youYou monitor yourself
Best forComplex conditions, full guidanceInformed self-researchers

Most of the research community operates through the gray market. The difference between a good and bad experience comes down to two things: independent testing and vendor selection.

Gray Market 101

The gray market is unregulated. That doesn't mean unsafe — it means you are the quality control.

Before you buy anything, internalize this:

You are your own regulator. No FDA, no BBB, and customer service is a Discord DM. If something goes wrong, you're the one who has to figure it out.
This isn't Amazon. Shipping could take 2-8 weeks. Chargebacks hurt everyone.
Protect your privacy. Use a throwaway email. The community is vulnerable to doxxing.
Test every order. HPLC testing is your only way to verify what's in the vial.

Which brings us to the most important question in the gray market:

Who do you actually trust?

Want a trusted vendor?

Get paired with one of our vetted shops.

The PepQuiz matches you with trusted vendors based on your peptide, budget, and experience level.

Find My Vendor

Now let's talk about what supplies you need.

Peptide Grocery List

🧪
Peptide Vial

Small glass bottle containing the peptide.

🧴
Alcohol Swabs

Used for sterilizing the vial and injection site.

💉
Mixing Syringe

Used to draw up BAC water and mix with peptide.

🖥
Clean Workspace

A designated area for preparing and injecting peptides.

💧
BAC or Sterile Water

Bacteriostatic water to reconstitute the peptide powder.

💉
Insulin Syringes

29-31 gauge needles for subcutaneous injection.

⚠
Sharps Container

For safe disposal of used needles and syringes.

Disclaimer: This content is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not medical advice. Peptides mentioned here are discussed in the context of published research and community experience. Always consult a licensed healthcare provider before starting any new compound. Professor Peptides does not sell peptides or any controlled substances. We are an educational platform.

Peptides mentioned here are discussed in the context of published research and community experience. Always consult a licensed healthcare provider before starting any new compound. Professor Peptides does not sell peptides or any controlled substances. We are an educational platform.

Professor Peptides Presents

Peptides For Dummiesddddddddddd

The 2026 Edition

By Professor PeptidesUpdated Feb 202615 min read

Everything you need to know about peptides in one place. No PhD required. No sales pitch. Just the straight facts.

TL;DR — Peptide Tier List

Ranked by community results, research depth, and real-world reliability.

TierPeptidesWhy
SBPC-157, GHK-Cu, Thymosin Beta-4Proven track record, broad benefits, well-tolerated
ACJC-1295/Ipamorelin, Semaglutide, PT-141Highly effective for specific goals, solid research
BSelank, Semax, KPV, EpithalonStrong community feedback, emerging clinical data
CDSIP, Melanotan II, AOD-9604Works for some, mixed results or notable side effects
DIGF-1 LR3, Follistatin, GHRP-6Higher risk, limited data, or better alternatives exist

Table of Contents

  1. TL;DR — Peptide Tier List
  2. Before Taking Peptides
  3. What Are Peptides?
  4. Why Your Body Needs Help
  5. How They Help You
  6. Can You Combine Them?
  7. How Long to Run It
  8. Myths, Busted
  9. How to Get Them
  10. Gray Market 101
  11. Peptide Grocery List

Before Taking Peptides

Peptides are powerful, but they're not a shortcut. They amplify what you're already doing right. If your foundation is broken, peptides won't fix it — they'll just amplify the mess. Get these right first:

🛏
Sleep

7-9 hours of quality sleep. This is when your body does most of its repair work. Peptides that boost GH release are largely useless if you're not sleeping.

🥦
Nutrition

Whole foods, adequate protein (0.8-1g per lb), and enough calories. You can't signal repair in a body that has no building materials.

🏋
Movement

Resistance training 3-4x per week minimum. Peptides amplify your training response — no training means nothing to amplify.

💧
Hydration

Half your bodyweight in ounces daily. Dehydration impairs every biological process peptides are trying to enhance.

You don't have to be perfect. But if you're sleeping 4 hours, living on fast food, and never moving your body — a peptide isn't the answer. Fix the basics first, then layer peptides on top.

What Are Peptides?

A peptide is a short chain of amino acids — typically between 2 and 50. They're smaller than proteins and act as signaling molecules, telling your body to do specific things like repair tissue, burn fat, or produce growth hormone.

Your body already makes peptides naturally. When you take one, you're amplifying a signal your body already recognizes — that's why they tend to work with your biology instead of against it.

Amino AcidPeptideProtein
SizeSingle molecule2-50 amino acids50+ amino acids
FunctionBuilding blockSignaling moleculeStructural / enzymatic
ExampleL-GlutamineBPC-157Collagen
How it worksProvides raw materialTells body what to doDoes the heavy lifting

Why Your Body Needs Help

As you age, your body makes fewer peptides naturally. Here's what that decline looks like:

20s

Peak production. GH, collagen, and repair peptides at all-time highs. Recovery is fast. Energy is abundant.

30s

Production starts declining ~1-2% per year. Recovery slows. Sleep quality drops. First signs of aging appear.

40s

Significant decline. GH output drops 50%+. Injuries take longer to heal. Fat accumulates easier. Muscle harder to maintain.

55+

Steep decline across all peptide pathways. Immune function weakens. Skin thins. Bone density drops. This is where targeted peptides make the biggest difference.

How They Help You

Peptides target specific pathways. Here are the 8 most common goals:

Anti-Aging

GHK-Cu, Epithalon, BPC-157

Injury Repair

BPC-157, TB-500, GHK-Cu

Fat Loss

Semaglutide, AOD-9604, Tesamorelin

Energy & Focus

Semax, Selank, Dihexa

Performance

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, MK-677, Follistatin

Gut Health

BPC-157, KPV, Larazotide

Better Skin

GHK-Cu, Copper Peptides, Collagen Peptides

Hormones

CJC-1295/Ipamorelin, PT-141, Kisspeptin

Find Your Peptide

Take the PepQuiz to find the right peptide for your specific goal. 4 minutes. No account needed.

Take the PepQuiz

Can You Combine Them?

Yes — and most experienced users do. But there's a right way and a wrong way. Here are the three main approaches:

MethodWhat It IsBest For
One PeptideSingle peptide, one goal at a timeBeginners, first-timers, specific issue
BlendPre-mixed combo in one vial (e.g., CJC/Ipa)Convenience, synergistic pairs
StackMultiple separate peptides, custom protocolExperienced users, multi-goal protocols

How Long to Run It

Peptides aren't meant to be taken forever. Most are run in cycles. Here are the terms you need to know:

TermDefinition
On-cycleThe period you're actively using a peptide. Typically 4-12 weeks depending on the compound.
Off-cycleThe break between cycles. Lets your body reset receptors and prevents desensitization. Usually 2-4 weeks.
TaperingGradually lowering your dose before stopping instead of quitting cold turkey. Reduces rebound effects.

Every peptide has its own recommended cycle length. The PepQuiz results include specific dosing and cycling info for your matched peptide.

Get Your Personalized Dosing Schedule

Take the quiz and we'll build a custom protocol based on your goals and experience level.

Get My Protocol

Myths, Busted

Let's clear up the biggest misconceptions about peptides.

MYTH"They work instantly"
FACTExpect 4-8 weeks of consistent use
MYTH"They're all safe"
FACTSide effects are possible
MYTH"They all do the same thing"
FACTEach targets different pathways
MYTH"Only for bodybuilders"
FACTMultiple health solutions
MYTH"Short-term use only"
FACTMany are safe long-term with cycling
MYTH"Injections are the only option"
FACTAlso: oral, nasal, and topical forms

How to Get Them

For years, doctors could prescribe most popular peptides. Licensed compounding pharmacies would make them for individual patients. It was legitimate, monitored, and it worked.

Then in 2023, the FDA placed the majority of popular peptides onto a restricted list — prohibiting licensed pharmacies from making them. Here's why:

  • FDA approval requires large-scale human clinical trials — a process that takes years and costs hundreds of millions of dollars
  • Most peptides are naturally occurring — making them difficult to patent
  • Without a patent, there's limited financial incentive to fund those trials
  • Without trial data, the FDA can't approve it — and the cycle continues

That one ruling created a gap. And the gray market filled it.

Your Two Options Today

A very short list of FDA-approved peptides you can get through a doctor. And everything else.

🏥 Licensed Physician🔍 Gray Market
Cost$300 to $600+/mo5-20x cheaper
SelectionLimited by FDAMuch wider
QualityPharmaceutical-gradeVaries by vendor
OversightDoctor monitors youYou monitor yourself
Best forComplex conditions, full guidanceInformed self-researchers

Most of the research community operates through the gray market. The difference between a good and bad experience comes down to two things: independent testing and vendor selection.

Gray Market 101

The gray market is unregulated. That doesn't mean unsafe — it means you are the quality control.

Before you buy anything, internalize this:

You are your own regulator. No FDA, no BBB, and customer service is a Discord DM. If something goes wrong, you're the one who has to figure it out.
This isn't Amazon. Shipping could take 2-8 weeks. Chargebacks hurt everyone.
Protect your privacy. Use a throwaway email. The community is vulnerable to doxxing.
Test every order. HPLC testing is your only way to verify what's in the vial.

Which brings us to the most important question in the gray market:

Who do you actually trust?

Want a trusted vendor?

Get paired with one of our vetted shops.

The PepQuiz matches you with trusted vendors based on your peptide, budget, and experience level.

Find My Vendor

Now let's talk about what supplies you need.

Peptide Grocery List

🧪
Peptide Vial

Small glass bottle containing the peptide.

🧴
Alcohol Swabs

Used for sterilizing the vial and injection site.

💉
Mixing Syringe

Used to draw up BAC water and mix with peptide.

🖥
Clean Workspace

A designated area for preparing and injecting peptides.

💧
BAC or Sterile Water

Bacteriostatic water to reconstitute the peptide powder.

💉
Insulin Syringes

29-31 gauge needles for subcutaneous injection.

⚠
Sharps Container

For safe disposal of used needles and syringes.
